sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Jacqueline, a 3-year mountain biker, fell and twisted her right ankle outward during a mountain biking lesson, resulting in pain, swelling, and bruising on the outer ankle.
She reports numbness and tingling in her toes, particularly in her heart, but this is not a concern for me as an orthopedic specialist.
She has been taking Tylenol for pain but has not found relief.
She has a fever, which she reports as 200 degrees Fahrenheit, but I have measured her temperature at 98.6 degrees Fahrenheit.
She is limping and has difficulty moving her ankle.
Past medical history: [NOT MENTIONED]
Review of systems: The patient reports numbness and tingling in her toes, particularly in her heart, but this is not a concern for me as an orthopedic specialist.
Current medications: I prescribed Tylenol for pain.
Objective:
Vital signs: Blood pressure is 118/77, heart rate is 77 beats per minute, and temperature is 98.6 degrees Fahrenheit.
There is no fever.
Physical exam: On the right ankle, there is edema and erythema on the lateral malleolus, associated ecchymosis, and a few excoriations.
There is pain to palpation of the right lateral malleolus, decreased flexion and extension of the right ankle, and internal and external rotation.
There is no apparent injury to the knee or the foot.
Test Results: The right ankle x-ray shows no acute fracture or bony abnormality, but there is some soft tissue injury.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: I believe the patient has an acute right ankle sprain.
Problem list: Ankle sprain; status: confirmed.
Plan:
I am going to put the patient in an air cast, give her crutches, and prescribe meloxicam 15 milligrams once a day for pain.
I recommend icing the area for 20 minutes, 5 times a day, and weight-bearing as tolerated.
I anticipate the patient to start feeling better in the next couple of weeks and will consider physical therapy or further imaging if she does not improve.
Follow-up: The patient will return in 2 weeks to re-evaluate her condition.
